name: Brittney R. Villalva
recommendation: What did they do well on?: Love that they're comfortable using Figma variable for controlling/maintaining strings. Appreciated the little work she had done on the consent screen. Shows real interest. Clearly using AI to research problems, create first drafts. Thinks about systems and user expectations/needs – overall solid product muscle. What did they do poorly on?: Only one case study – seems like a big miss. Would have wanted to see a lot more. Didn't talk a lot about design partners, how they collaborate. Didn't get into the details on the why behind certain words. Would have wanted a deeper dive into their craft rather than just a high level overview.

name: Christie Fremon
recommendation: Portfolio: I'm a high 3 – the work and experience feel very solid and would warrant a 4, but the signal I got around humility and collaboration makes me doubt a little bit. I'll try to dive into those in my 1:1 if I can. Principles: Christie is a rock-solid content designer, with a ton of experience, is hard-working, very passionate and driven, curious and opinionated. They'll bring a ton of energy and will be an active voice on the team. It'll be on us to make sure we set them up for success and help channel that energy the right way, but I'm confident they'll have a lot of impact at Plaid.

name: Christine Nedilsky
recommendation: I wish we had seen a little more in this presentation – All of my signals feel a little weak, but the little I got was all positive.

name: Jacquelynn Gothard
recommendation: I'm a soft no hire – nothing I heard was a huge red flag, but I did come out of this conversation not feeling very confident that Jacque would be successful at Plaid as the sole content designer (for a little while) and setting the right bar for future hires.

name: Josh Stein
recommendation: Portfolio: Super strong presentation. Great work, great delivery, and relevant experience. Probably more of a mid-high D5. Principles: Great candidate. Maybe a bit over-experienced for the role as we've set it up – but would be immediately able to contribute in a meaningful way.

name: Luke Maffei
recommendation: Veryyyy soft hire. No huge red flag, but nothing really standing up either, except how their first case study was centered around design principles, which I found well framed and interesting.

name: Sinjin Hilaski
recommendation: 1:1: C did well in this interview and seems aligned with our company values (though we only had 30 min so I couldn't go super deep). Portfolio: I'm overall a VERY soft Yes on the portfolio presentation, mostly because the candidate's work seems pretty solid, and their experience is quite relevant. However, I don't think they did amazingly well with their storytelling, and missed several opportunities to better context-set, show broad explorations, articulate their work and mention business needs. Caveat: I don't feel confident about their craft and execution. I'm a soft Yes if others had a solid signal on it and feel positive, if not I'm a No.

name: Stephen R. Fox
recommendation: Portfolio: Huge bummer of a portfolio review – the candidate chose to only show their work for 15 min, and didn't go in any depth. Disappointing given the experience they have and the type of work they've done. Based solely on this portfolio review, I'm a strong no. 1:1: Based on this interview alone, I'd be a soft 3. This C is overall aligned with our company values, though I'm not feeling very strongly about their capacity to self-drive their impact.

name: Caitlin Scott
recommendation: I'm not really well calibrated on this role (this is the first candidate I see) so this is a very soft 2. I wish Caitlin had shared more details about how she works with Sonos' guidelines, or how she writes for particular audiences, or what tone she's trying to match. But I'm also not going to be a blocker if others have stronger POVs.

name: Bethany Valoe
recommendation: Portfolio: I'm a high 3 – there was lots to like, especially in the first case study.

name: Geoffrey Kim
recommendation: Principles: Geoff is a lovely human being – but I'm really struggling to see them successful in this role. The gap between their existing skillset, interest, and approach to the work, and our expectations for the role and on our Design team is just too wide. Portfolio: I'm struggling to put a 3 here, because there just isn't enough that felt convincing. Sometimes the work didn't feel super fleshed out, sometimes the process or role wasn't super well articulated, and it's unclear to me whether C could easily build campaigns for us outside of video ones.

name: Kiran Puri
recommendation: Principles: I came out of this interview a very soft 3. I had a very pleasant chat with Kiran, heard promising answers, that were at times contrasted with small red flags. Overall, no big red flags but I also don't feel strongly that this candidate would be a huge addition to our team. Portfolio: I'm a soft 3. The relevant experience is a plus, and the execution skills look solid – I was just craving a bit more to feel more strongly.

name: Andrea Baltazar
ratings: 2 - No Hire (interview focuses), 2 - No Hire (portfolio), 2 - No Hire (principles, shadowing Rubi Santoro)
recommendation: Interview Focuses: I didn't get a ton of signal in this interview (C tended to ramble a bit) but what I got didn't make me feel confident they'd succeed in this role. Nothing was terrible, but very little stood out as a key strength. Portfolio: Though this candidate has clear experience and strengths, I didn't see enough depth in how they lead, in the scope of projects they can support, in the impact they can have, and how they can raise the quality bar on a team.

name: Adam Kennedy
recommendation: Seems solid (at least based off the 30 min chat we had). Would love to see their portfolio review (if we do one). Would make a great partner to Product Design.

name: Jason Rosenberg
recommendation: I'm a very, very soft hire – if Chris is impressed with Jason as a leader and feels confident that they'll lead the team successfully in this new phase, I won't block us from moving to the next stage. I feel sufficiently confident that I can work with Jason well in nurturing the Product <> Brand culture (though I'll likely have to lean into it a lot more) but have a few concerns I'd want us to check into in future rounds if we move forward with Jason.

name: Jennifer Hung
recommendation: Overall I'm a soft hire. No red flags, but also nothing making me super excited from this quick conversation. Their motion design skills/experience, combined with business mind and process obsession would all be helpful adds, but I don't yet have strong confidence they'd uplevel the work of our team overall.

name: Shannon Riley
ratings: 2 - No Hire (shadowing Rubi Santoro), 2 - No Hire (interview focuses)
recommendation: Interview Focuses: I didn't get a ton of signal in this interview (C tended to ramble a bit with their answers, which limited the number of questions I was able to ask) but what I got didn't make me feel confident they'd succeed in this role. Nothing was terrible, but very little stood out as a key strength they'd be adding to the team.

name: Brady Voss
recommendation: Pre-screen: I've personally worked with Brady for a few years at Facebook. Brady is extremely talented, approachable and engaging, and has many years of experience shipping large-scale products with a high level of polish. Fantastic consumer-facing track-record, great craft, solid leadership experience, management and mentorship experience, great collaborator. Only open question is how well they will adapt to a fast-pace, scrappy environment like Plaid. Portfolio: Very disappointed in Brady's portfolio presentation. Presentation wasn't engaging, most slides were too text-heavy, content was focused on the wrong aspects of the work. This presentation highlighted that Brady is well-tuned for communicating within a huge organization but may not be able to adapt their framing to a more focused audience. Based on this presentation, I'm not confident Brady can apply his experience and skillset to Plaid and be successful.

name: Chris Sanchez
recommendation: I don't think Chris is a strong enough candidate for this role.

name: John Bai
ratings: 4 - Strong Hire (pre-screen), 4 - Strong Hire (portfolio), 4 - Strong Hire (final round)
recommendation: Pre-screen: John is great. Let's bring them to the next step! Fintech experience at Brex, great consumer-facing experience at Lyft and Dropbox, very solid craft, great prototyper using Framer, solid business acumen, great communicator, curious and thoughtful. Portfolio: Fintech experience, very solid craft, great prototyper, solid business acumen and user centricity. Final Round: John is overall great. They're earlier in the level than some other candidates we've seen, but their growth areas are clear and easily coachable. I already can picture where they'd plug in on day 1 to be successful. Great startup mentality, great communicator, great self-awareness.

name: Wesley Haines
recommendation: Wes would be a great candidate if we were looking at a high D5/early D6 role. But his profile is too similar to one we already have on the team and I'm not confident he could succeed in the role we're hiring for.

name: Cassandra Lim
recommendation: Principles: This is a soft no, because I don't think this candidate is quite ready for the work we have in the pipeline for them. They'd be most effective in a small product team, owning a fairly well understood problem space, but still have to grow their ability to collaborate with other designers on bigger challenges. Portfolio: I'm a very soft 3. No bad signal, but also not blown away by anything. This candidate seems early in their career, and more of a D4 with solid potential than an established D5.

name: Dominic Salbatera
recommendation: Portfolio: Too many holes in this candidate's skillset. Their storytelling and product thinking are solid, but the rest of the skillset we need simply isn't there.

name: Ivan Marinkovic
recommendation: Very easy to talk to, articulate, friendly, thoughtful. Very relevant experience at Wise. Has a bit of management experience. Has worked as a content designer. Seems self-aware and thoughtful. Actual product design experience is a bit on the shorter hand (just above two years), but their previous roles in Content Design have given them the opportunity to build a robust skillset. Let's bring Ivan for the final round!

name: Jason Martell
recommendation: Pre-screen: Lot of Fintech experience, super solid prototyper, 'Show, don't tell' approach, mentorship/teaching experience, good energy. Room for growth in XFN-collaboration. Portfolio: I'm a soft hire on Jason. Their skillset matches well our 'The Hybrid' D7 archetype – someone with strong technical craft and execution skills. However, very little signal on business acumen, and their presentation lacked structure. Final Round: Jason is a passionate designer, with many years of experience, including in Fintech, and a great appetite for Plaid. Their hard skills are great. While we won't find the perfect unicorn, this candidate's T-shaped skillset leans too heavily on their hard skills. Not very humble, struggle to collaborate, still growing their leadership skills. I'm not confident they'd be successful in this role.

name: Judy Zhong
recommendation: Principles: Judy seems to have a really solid all-around skillset combined with very strong growth potential. She seems to have learned a ton in the few years of experience she's had so far, and demonstrated clear appetite to continue growing and tackling bigger problems while sharpening her skills. Portfolio: Might be on the earlier side of IC5 given the years of experience, but really solid candidate with a great all-around skillset, and growth potential.

name: Justin Kim
recommendation: Portfolio: Justin is way too junior for what we're looking for in this role. Very minimal experience, storytelling showed lack of mature product thinking, demos/prototypes were very basic, answers were very rambly and hand-wavy. Principles: Very hand-wavy answers, did not really own up their mistakes, their way of balancing business impact and user needs is very much still developing, have mostly operated as sole designer.

name: Lilian Tellez
recommendation: Principles: Struggling to give a rating. Got very low signal on many dimensions. Their answers were a little too surface-level. Don't have strong conviction this C would help uplevel the team. Portfolio: Very low energy when presenting. Seems to lack strong POV/ambition with their design. Presentation skills are fairly important at Plaid – this is the second time I get to see this C present this work in the last 12 months, and had the same notes the first time, which is concerning.

name: Meghan O'Donnell
recommendation: Principles: Good storyteller, cares about craft and systems, acts as an owner, data driven approach. But don't seem to have a lot of 'deep product design' experience. Portfolio: Promising candidate, who seems smart and confident in their process – but who is way too early in their journey for our roles and the level of autonomy we expect. They'd be a good bet if we were hiring a mid-level IC4, but I don't see the signs of crazy fast growth that'd get them to the level we need in 6-12 months.

name: Melisa Ashrafy
recommendation: Principles: Based on this interview alone, this candidate shows a lot of the values we look for at Plaid. I still think they're a bit on the junior end, however, but I'm willing to discuss hiring them if we see enough spikes. Portfolio: If this role was a D4 role, the spikes I see on process, communication and the clear interest in building design systems would make them a solid candidate. Since we're currently thinking about a more experienced D5, I'll have to be a soft no.

name: Misaki Suehiro
recommendation: Principles: The work at Shopify on B2B platform was impactful. Shared a good story about advocating for the right implementation. Portfolio: Case studies felt shallow, both about the same project. Unclear where UX Lead role started/ended. Answers could have been more succinct. Contributions seemed very small in scope. Small note: they were let go by Spotify 10 months ago and still looking, which isn't super encouraging.

name: Paul Kim
recommendation: Principles: I'm a very soft 2. Didn't get super crisp signal, but the little I got made me a bit nervous about this candidate's likeliness to be successful at Plaid and achieve ambitious goals, at fast pace. Don't seem very ambitious – literally asked 'In what ways do you think your team is too ambitious?' Portfolio: This was a strong presentation. Feeling much better about this candidate after having seen this presentation, which gave me better signal on some of my areas of concern. Clear presenter, great intentionality, ambitious project grounded in business goals with huge impact.

name: Pradeep Periasamy
recommendation: Principles: C doesn't really own their mistakes or credit their partners. Didn't demonstrate ability to raise the bar. Lacked ambition. Didn't really show how much they care about sweating the details. Said a lot of platitudes that lacked concrete examples. Portfolio: Experience mostly limited to 1 company with small design team. Didn't mention partners. Questionable use of Personas. Surface-level intentionality behind certain design decisions.

name: Renaise Kim
recommendation: Principles: If this were an IC4 role, I'd be a 3. When considering this being a high IC5 role, I'm a 2. Portfolio: Though pretty creative and comfortable designing 0-1 products, candidate seems very junior in their process, product thinking, business acumen, collaboration skills and presentation skills. Too fresh for what we're looking for. My sense is that Renaise is a mid-level IC4.

name: Rita Kutner
recommendation: Principles: Communication style felt pretty scattered, which at times made it hard to follow. Seem to carry resentment when a decision doesn't go their way. Portfolio: Based on this presentation alone, I'm a no-hire. Nothing was a red flag, but I also didn't feel enthusiastic about anything I heard, except that the candidate has experience in Fintech. Storytelling felt scattered and unprepared.

name: Stav Tobin
recommendation: Principles: No red flags, and no super green flags. Candidate seems solid overall. Portfolio: No red flags in this interview, but also nothing that particularly stood out and made me particularly excited about what this C could bring to the team.

name: Stephanie Park
recommendation: Principles: Candidate is too fresh to be a bar raiser on the team, and I don't feel confident we have the right setup for them to quickly ramp up and be successful in an autonomous way. Portfolio: Promising candidate, who seems smart and confident in their process – but who is way too early in their journey for our roles and the level of autonomy we expect from our designers. They'd be a good bet if we were hiring a mid-level IC4.

name: Paul Persky
recommendation: Robust experience, working in different setups, in fast-growing or fast-moving environments. Strong user centricity. Founder/owner mentality. Lot of mentorship experience. Seems to have solid leadership chops (stakeholder management, etc). Actual product design experience is a bit on the shorter hand (just above two years), but their previous roles in Content Design have given them the opportunity to build a robust skillset.

name: Charlene Tsang
recommendation: Principles: I'm not confident Charlene would raise the bar on our team. Craft is ok, but not impressive. No technical skills or motion design skills. Portfolio (note: feedback was submitted in the wrong form): During our chat, the candidate never talked about business goals or audience needs. A lot of the answers felt very shallow. Didn't display a great ownership mentality. Overall underwhelmed by this candidate, who seemed promising on paper but whose answers felt very surface-level and uninspiring.

name: Elliott Hutchison
recommendation: Portfolio: Craft looks solid (after we got a chance to see some explorations). Transparent and humble. Once we asked for explorations, we saw really cool work. But not a very engaging presenter. Presentation lacked content. First case study was 15 min long, second was 5 min long. Felt like the candidate really hadn't prepared for it. Overall nothing that gets me excited enough to compensate for what feels like lack of preparation/care for the interview.

name: Icy Leng
recommendation: Portfolio: Good concepting and execution. Seem pretty scrappy. Good interaction/motion design. But not great at framing intentionality/decision-making – arguments seemed shallow. No mentions of design system. Goals seemed hand-wavy. Didn't see a lot of wide explorations. There are some spikes (pretty technical), but overall work lacks intentionality, divergence, wasn't grounded in business goals or metrics. Don't feel confident this C would hit the ground running. Principles: Pretty broad skillset, able to work fast, seem to care about craft. But background is only in small startups, haven't developed the 'championship team' muscle, still growing strategic skills, don't seem very impact oriented. A lot of the answers felt very shallow. Based solely on this interview, I don't feel confident that this candidate can raise the bar on the team.

name: Paul Mathisen
recommendation: Portfolio: Very organized. Clean process, always thinking about reusability and usability of his work, great documentation. Good ideas for micro-interactions. But GoPro project didn't feel like the type of design project we do on Web – felt more like a Production design project. Craft is ok, but doesn't stand out as a strength. Hasn't dealt with a lot of ambiguity. No mention of stakeholder management. I don't think Paul showed us that he's got quite the experience we're looking for for this role. His sense of organization and ability to work within constraints is great, but I haven't seen anything that makes me confident Paul would be raising the bar and having high impact on our web team.

name: Sarah Cantrell
recommendation: Portfolio: Some decent motion design experience/skills. Jack of all trades, able to handle the rebrand of a smaller company on their own and work directly with C-level executives. Clearly articulated goals. Explored diligently and shared some of their explorations. But craft isn't meeting our bar. Lack of attention to details throughout, including in presentation that had typos.
